name: Gorgias
one_liner: Ecommerce-focused customer support helpdesk that centralizes tickets, chat, and automation across channels.
access_notes: Gorgias is a paid SaaS (with a free trial). Once you have a helpdesk account, REST API credentials (base URL + email + API key) are generated instantly and self-serve from Settings > Account > REST API. API-key/Basic auth is for private apps; OAuth2 is required for building public apps. No human approval needed to obtain working credentials.
api_notes: Well-documented REST API (developers.gorgias.com) with predictable resource-oriented URLs, JSON bodies/responses. Covers tickets, messages, customers, tags, custom fields, macros, rules, views, teams/users, voice calls/recordings, events, surveys, integrations/widgets, and account settings — roughly 100+ endpoints.
mcp_notes: Gorgias ships a first-party remote MCP server (open beta since May 2026) at https://mcp.gorgias.com/mcp. Included at no extra cost on every Helpdesk plan; connects to Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible client. Community MCP servers (e.g. mattcoatsworth on GitHub, Pipedream, Zapier) also exist.
main_blocker: Requires a Gorgias account (paid SaaS, free trial available); API credentials themselves are instant and self-serve once signed in.

evidence.claim: Gorgias uses HTTP Basic Auth (base API URL + email + API key) for private apps; API keys are created self-serve in Settings > Account > REST API.
Public apps must use OAuth2 authentication.
Gorgias API is a REST API with resource-oriented URLs, JSON encoding, and standard HTTP codes.
Gorgias MCP is a first-party server included at no extra cost on any plan, connectable via https://mcp.gorgias.com/mcp.

open_questions: Exact minimum plan/trial constraints for API access were not individually verified; webhook/event support is confirmed via HTTP integrations and the Event object but the full webhook subscription model was not deeply inspected.
